hero: Mexico City is colour, courtyards, ten-peso tacos, and the friendliest 7am you'll ever experience. Show up slow, leave late.

sections.title: Neighbourhoods to know
sections.body:
Roma Norte for art-deco buildings and good coffee.
Condesa for jacarandas and tree-shaded brunches.
Coyoacán for Sunday markets and Frida Kahlo's blue house.

sections.title: Getting around
sections.body:
Uber/Didi work everywhere; cheaper than a metro day-pass for short hops.
Metro is fast but very busy; women-only carriages at the front during rush.
Ecobici day passes are great for Roma/Condesa loops.

sections.title: Eat
sections.body:
Tacos al pastor: El Vilsito (after 8pm only).
Breakfast: Lalo! in Roma.
Mezcal: La Clandestina.
Mercado treats: Mercado Roma for a full afternoon.

sections.title: Local customs
sections.body:
10% tip is normal; 15% is generous.
Lunch is the big meal (14:00–16:00).
"Provecho" said when leaving a restaurant — say it back, you'll fit right in.

sections.title: If something goes wrong
sections.body:
Emergency 911.
Tourist police (English): 5658-1111.
International hospital: ABC Santa Fe +52 55 5230 8000.
